summary:
Adrenaline, amiodarone, and vasopressin are recommended by AHA guidelines during cardiac arrest; however, atropine is not routinely used due to its limited efficacy outside certain specialized scenarios. \boxed{atropine}

The summary captures that only one option was considered non-recommended according to the guideline while highlighting why it stands out from others mentioned as part of typical treatment protocol. This aligns with focusing solely on critical information pertinent directly answering whether any particular medication should be excluded based upon provided guidance criteria alone.
